extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won a sprint stage where echelons formed immediately in crosswinds but never fully split the peloton after the chaotic opening kilometers.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quick-Step positioned Asgreen second wheel immediately at race start, exploiting crosswinds before GC teams organized counterattacks.
GC favorites failed to organize echelon attacks despite Pogačar having few teammates forward, suggesting implicit non-aggression pact.
Quintana potentially soft-pedaled finale tactically since losing time meaningless for polka-dot defense, prioritizing recovery over positioning.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: Caught deep in peloton (40th position) during opening echelon splits with depleted team support, yet GC rivals never capitalized.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Pre-race stated crosswind stages are easiest for his positioning skills, demonstrating tactical confidence beyond pure sprinting.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: Dropped 16 minutes behind Philipsen but may have strategically abandoned positioning since time losses don't threaten KOM jersey lead.
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: First significant rider caught out (number 201) when echelons formed, indicating poor pre-stage positioning awareness.
rider_signals.ASGREEN Casper: positive: Positioned second wheel immediately as crosswinds hit, executing perfect leadout domestique placement for Quick-Step's sprint ambitions.
